# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Continuous Deployment to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Build Project
      run: |
        echo "Building the project..."
        # Replace with your actual build commands
        # npm ci
        # npm run build
        echo "Project built successfully."

    - name: Run Tests
      run: |
        echo "Running tests..."
        # Replace with your actual test commands
        # npm test
        echo "Tests passed."

    - name: Deploy to Production
      run: |
        echo "Initiating deployment to production environment..."
        # Replace with your actual production deployment commands
        # For example:
        # - aws s3 sync ./dist s3://your-production-bucket
        # - kubectl apply -f kubernetes/production-deployment.yaml
        # - ssh user@your-production-server 'cd /app && git pull && pm2 restart app'
        echo "Deployment to production completed successfully!"
      env:
        # Example: Pass necessary secrets for your deployment
        # AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
        # AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
        # KUBECONFIG: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG }}
        # PRODUCTION_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_API_KEY }}
        # Add your production specific environment variables and secrets here.
        EXAMPLE_SECRET: ${{ secrets.EXAMPLE_SECRET }}
